header.headline: Registering Vital Events in Hungary
header.description: Birth, marriage, divorce, and death — any life event involving Hungarian citizens may require registration with Hungarian civil authorities. Here's what the Honorary Consul can help with.

intro: Hungarian citizens living abroad are required to register major life events (birth, marriage, divorce, death) with Hungarian civil registry authorities. The Honorary Consul can authenticate the American documents needed to support these registrations.

birth.name: Birth Registration
birth.body: A child born abroad to a Hungarian parent must be registered in the Hungarian civil registry. The Honorary Consul authenticates American birth certificates and supporting documents for submission to Hungarian authorities.
whatToBring: Parents' Hungarian passports or ID cards

marriage.name: Marriage Registration
marriage.body: Marriages abroad involving Hungarian citizens must be registered in Hungary. The Honorary Consul authenticates American marriage certificates and related documents.
whatToBring: Original American marriage certificate (apostilled if available)
Both spouses' valid ID or passport
Divorce decree if either party was previously married
Cash or check for authentication fees

divorce.name: Divorce Registration
divorce.body: Divorce decrees from American courts must be authenticated for recognition by Hungarian authorities. The Honorary Consul can authenticate the American court order.
whatToBring: Final divorce decree from the American court
Cash or check for authentication fees

death.name: Death Registration
death.body: The death of a Hungarian citizen abroad must be reported to Hungarian authorities. In addition to authentication, the Honorary Consul can assist with emergency coordination — contacting next of kin, the Embassy, and relevant agencies.
whatToBring: Deceased's Hungarian passport or national ID
Cash or check for authentication fees

passport.name: Passport-Related Documentation
passport.body: Hungarian passport renewals and issuance require biometric equipment available only at the Consulate General in New York. The Honorary Consul can authenticate supporting documents and explain the process.
whatToBring: Contact the NY Consulate General directly for passport appointments

cta.heading: Need to Register a Vital Event?
cta.body: Book an appointment to authenticate supporting documents and receive guidance on the registration process.
